title: How Polestar CEO Michael Lohscheller Is Steering the EV Brand Toward Profitability
text: CEO Michael Lohscheller has laid out a vision defined by patience, design conviction, and sustainability to differentiate Polestar in an era where simply being electric is no longer enough. Lohscheller estimates the brand needs to surpass 100,000 units in annual sales to reach profitability. Brand Positioning: We don't do cars for everybody. Some of our cars, like the Polestar 4, doesn't have a rear window. Not everybody loves it, but a lot of people love it and that's why the car is very successful. Lohscheller emphasizes sustainability and environmental commitment: We use renewable energies. We brought the CO2 emissions down in our cars by 25% the last four years. Polestar has been advancing its Polestar 0 project, an ambitious initiative aiming to produce a truly climate-neutral car by 2030. Lohscheller sees an opportunity to attract disgruntled Tesla owners, having told his salespeople to specifically target such individuals. The strategy focuses on high-margin, design-conscious EVs that differentiate on sustainability and customer experience rather than price competition.
